title: Tetraria nigrovaginata
taxonomy: Tetraria nigrovaginata is classified in the family Cyperaceae. This family belongs to the order Poales. The class is Liliopsida, also known as monocots. The division is Spermatophyta, which is the group of vascular plants with seeds. The kingdom is Plantae, which includes all flowering plants.

parts: Tetraria nigrovaginata, also known as the black sheath rush, has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. intro: Tetraria nigrovaginata, also known as the black sheathed tetraria, is a species of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, which can be used to treat various ailments such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Tetraria nigrovaginata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include decoctions and infusions, often used to treat skin conditions, coughs, and colds.
